title: Who won the 2025-26 Liga Portugal (Portuguese top-flight football)?
rules: If SC Braga is the 2025-26 Liga Portugal Champion, then the market resolves to Yes.
tavily_brief: [redacted] Benfica sits third with 75 points from 31 matches. SC Braga occupies fourth place with 56 points from 32 matches, mathematically eliminated from title contention. Multiple forecasting models show Porto as overwhelming favorite. Estimador.pt's 50,000 simulations (updated March 17, 2026) assigned Porto a 92% championship probability. Goallytics' 100,000 simulations (April 26, 2026) gave Porto 98.33% odds. Prediction markets on Polymarket and Lines.com showed Porto at 96-97% probability in late April. Betting odds reflected this dominance: Oddschecker listed Porto at 1/7, while Braga was priced at 4500/1. Porto's consistency has been exceptional—28 wins, 4 draws, and only 2 losses through 34 matches, with a +48 goal differential and just 18 goals conceded. The team controls its own destiny with limited matches remaining. Sporting CP would require Porto to collapse dramatically while winning all remaining fixtures to close the gap. Benfica, despite an unbeaten league record through March, remains too far back mathematically. SC Braga finished fourth with 58 points from 33 matches (16 wins, 10 draws, 7 losses), 27 points behind Porto. Their season focused on European competition (reaching the Europa League semi-finals) rather than the domestic title. Braga's probability of winning the league was effectively 0% across all major forecasting models by late April. **Most important facts:** (1) Porto held an insurmountable seven-point lead with only 2-3 matches remaining for most challengers; (2) All major forecasting models and betting markets assigned Porto 96%+ probability by late April 2026; (3) SC Braga finished fourth, 27 points behind the leader, with zero realistic path to the title.

args.reasoning:
Evidence inventory:
- STRONG: At end of 2025 (matchday 16), FC Porto led Liga Portugal with 46 points, 5 ahead of Sporting (41), 10 ahead of Benfica (36) (Bola na Rede). Porto continued as clear leader per A Bola Dec 27, 2025.
- STRONG: A Transfermarkt reference appearing to show end-of-season standings has FC Porto 1st with 85 points, Benfica 2nd (76), Sporting 3rd (76) after 32 matchdays. While the season has 34 matchdays, with a 9-point lead and only 2 games left, Porto would be virtually clinched or already champions.
- Kalshi prices: FC Porto YES@0.99, all others at ~0.01 or lower. The market views the title as essentially decided.

Implication: Multiple converging signals (mid-season lead of 5-10 pts, late-season lead of 9 pts with 2 games remaining) indicate FC Porto is the overwhelming favorite/likely already-confirmed champion of the 2025-26 Liga Portugal. Porto haven't won the league since 2021-22, and Benfica/Sporting have alternated titles, but evidence this season clearly points to Porto. Manager Francesco Farioli has reportedly turned them into a dominant force.

Upset check: A 9-point lead with 2 matchdays (6 points) remaining is mathematically clinched. Even if not fully clinched, the probability of Sporting/Benfica overhauling is near zero. No reports of scandals, deductions, or collapses.

Sanity: Base rate for league leader with 9pt lead and <3 games left winning ≈ 98%+. Aligns with Kalshi 0.99.

I'll set FC Porto at 0.96 (cap), distribute remaining ~0.04 mostly to Benfica/Sporting as the only mathematically possible challengers, with tiny residuals for others.

args.reasoning:
Evidence inventory: Strong — FC Porto’s official website published “Champions and Kings of Portugal” on 2026-05-02 stating “FC Porto have won their 31st league title,” explicitly noting they finished top of the 2025/26 league, earned direct UEFA Champions League qualification, and secured a place in the 2026/27 Super Cup (club site: fcporto.pt/news/20260502-en-champions-and-kings-of-portugal). This is a primary, named source with unambiguous language about the 2025/26 championship. Moderate — The same announcement highlights the club’s updated trophy count (“31st league title,” “most decorated in Portuguese football”), which is consistent with a completed season outcome rather than a provisional claim. This resolves all disagreement — the outliers (TOT_OPUS at 0.74, DEEP_0 at 0.74, DEEP_1 at 0.66) were excessively hedged given the confirmed outcome. The question is essentially resolved.

RESOLUTION via search: Confirmed the FC Porto official site published "Champions and Kings of Portugal" stating they won their 31st league title by finishing top of the 2025/26 league. Given the outcome is essentially confirmed by an official club announcement post-clinching, Porto's probability should be at or near the Kalshi price of 0.99.
